message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  The systematic identification of host genetic risk factors is essential for the understanding and treatment of COVID-19. By performing a meta-analysis of two independent genome-wide association (GWAS) summary datasets (N = 680,128), a novel locus at 21q22.11 was identified to be associated with COVID-19 infection (rs9976829 in
                  <jats:italic>IFNAR2</jats:italic>
                  and upstream of
                  <jats:italic>IL10RB</jats:italic>
                  , OR = 1.16, 95% CI = 1.09 - 1.23, P = 2.57×10
                  <jats:sup>−6</jats:sup>
                  ). The rs9976829 represents a strong splicing quantitative trait locus (sQTL) for both
                  <jats:italic>IFNAR2</jats:italic>
                  and
                  <jats:italic>IL10RB</jats:italic>
                  genes, especially in lung tissue (P 1.8×10
                  <jats:sup>−24</jats:sup>
                  ). Gene-based association analysis also found
                  <jats:italic>IFNAR2</jats:italic>
                  was significantly associated with COVID-19 infection (P = 2.58×10
                  <jats:sup>−7</jats:sup>
                  ). Integrative genomics analysis of combining GWAS with eQTL data showed the expression variations of
                  <jats:italic>IFNAR2</jats:italic>
                  and
                  <jats:italic>IL10RB</jats:italic>
                  have prominent effects on COVID-19 in various types of tissues, especially in lung tissue. The majority of
                  <jats:italic>IFNAR2</jats:italic>
                  -expressing cells were dendritic cells (40%) and plasmacytoid dendritic cells (38.5%), and
                  <jats:italic>IL10RB</jats:italic>
                  -expressing cells were mainly nonclassical monocytes (29.6%).
                  <jats:italic>IFNAR2</jats:italic>
                  and
                  <jats:italic>IL10RB</jats:italic>
                  are targeted by several interferons-related drugs. Together, our results uncover 21q22.11 as a novel susceptibility locus for COVID-19, in which individuals with G alleles of rs9976829 have a higher probability of COVID-19 susceptibility than those with non-G alleles.
                </jats:p>
title: Integrative Genomics Analysis Reveals a Novel 21q22.11 Locus Contributing to Susceptibility of COVID-19

reference.unstructured: Z. Wu , J. M. McGoogan , JAMA 2020.
